idea: baseline 8-step bf8 on ltx-perf-tip base (rebase done: audio 0.49s inherited)
notes: gen#1 warm Total 6.06s. ms_view 6060 -> goal 3000. Levers: L4 sparse attn (MSA reuse) + VAE accel -> ~5s; 3s needs Tier C 4-step distill (out of scope). 11-step ref tmp/default_ltxperf.mp4 (8.05s).

8-step bf8 vs bf8-11step ref = mean-PCC 0.747 (min 0.677) — LOWER than deffb9 fork 0.85; ltx-perf tip pipeline diverges more at 8-step. GATE re-frame: quality-NEUTRAL levers (VAE trace, host-bubble) must hold PCC>=0.98 vs the CURRENT 8-step output (8step_ltxperf.mp4); quality-AFFECTING levers (L4 sparse) gate vs 11-step >= ~0.74 (dont degrade the operating point). refs: bf8-11step=ref_bf8_11step.mp4, bf16-11step=default_ltxperf.mp4.

REJECT: byte-identical (PCC 1.000 vs current 8-step, quality-neutral confirmed) but NET-NEGATIVE: VAE 0.95->1.01s (+0.06s), Total 6.06->6.10s. VAE is device-COMPUTE-bound not host-dispatch-bound, so trace-replay overhead > dispatch savings (same lesson as BWE/audio traces). Code kept env-gated OFF (default byte-identical) as an off-by-default experimental. Real VAE win = Kevin C++ (np-halo/copy-free, kevinmi/np-halo-fabric-mux, 82 commits divergent) — integration decision, out of Python scope.

idea: L4 mask-search recalibrated on ltx-perf tip (M1)
notes: M1 DONE + replicates deffb9: at +-3f min-PCC>=0.85 holds at blocks 12/24/32/40/47, fails 4/8/16/20 (early attend globally). ~half windowable, training-free, block-specific. Map opt/l4/depth_map_ltxperftip.txt. Next = M2 ring K-skip kernel (days, reuse MSA) — the biggest quality-safe lever (-0.3-0.5s -> ~5.6s). Not a loop lap.

idea: L4 ring K-skip kernel design (M2 blueprint) + P0 go/no-go
notes: BLUEPRINT DONE (opt/l4/kernel_plan.md): modify ring SDPA kernel (not fork MSA — MSA op is in-tree but decode-shaped + content-indexer LTX doesnt need). Static temporal band → per-Q-chunk K-range skip, reuse the STANDARD sliding-window machinery in the RING path. BUT the P0 go/no-go likely KILLS the wall-clock win: band-skip saves SDPA compute (which SDPA-LoFi proved is fabric-HIDDEN, 0.06s wash) + local K/V fill, NOT the inter-device all-gather fabric bytes (the actual comms-bound bottleneck). So L4 is quality-viable but likely ~0 wall-clock here. LOOP CONVERGED: config levers exhausted, L4 fabric-neutralized, VAE trace reject. 3s (and ~5.6s) need Tier-C 4-step distill (training) or a fabric-bytes reduction (num_links=2 = BH wall). Kevin np-halo C++ is the one real VAE lever (integration).

idea: L4 verdict CORRECTION: windowing cuts COMMS via banded all-gather
notes: CORRECTED earlier wrong conclusion (user caught it). Verified: fused ring SDPA = full RingAttentionAllGatherAsync (all K/V->all devices) on dedicated AG cores concurrent w/ SDPA; active_ring_iter_mask skips SDPA consumption NOT AG fabric. So K-loop-skip doesnt cut comms BUT a BANDED all-gather (cap each K/V shards ring hops to +-w window) does -> ~2x less inter-device fabric = the real comms win on this comms-bound DiT. SDPA-LoFi wash confirms fabric-bound (compute hidden) -> attack fabric not compute. L4 REOPENS as a genuine wall-clock lever. Re-scoping kernel to the banded RingAttentionAllGatherAsync.
